title: Fix reordering recipes to use an insert method rather than swap method
excerpt:
The new ability to reorder integration recipes seems to work by swapping positions. 
If I move recipe 16 to recipe 3, recipe 3 becomes 16 not recipe 4. This means I have to move the new recipe 16 (formerly 3) to recipe 4…

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
The new ability to reorder integration recipes seems to work by swapping positions.

If I move recipe 16 to recipe 3, recipe 3 becomes 16 not recipe 4. This means I have to move the new recipe 16 (formerly 3) to recipe 4, 4 becomes 16, 16 has to now be moved to recipe 5 and so on for all the recipes. Just to move one recipes position.

This doesn’t seem viable at all.

I would think moving recipe 16 to recipe 3 would shift 3->4->5 and so on rather than just swap positions - but that doesn’t appear to be the case.

What am I doing wrong?

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml:
hi @anon29275264

Where did you find the ability to reorder the integration recipes? I can’t find that option.

posts.author: anon29275264 (anon29275264)
posts.cookedHtml:
Apps Framework

New: Reorder integration recipes

You can now reorder integration recipes!!! Just open your integration recipes, hit "Reorder" and drag and drop :) Use this to improve your new users' experience – put your app's most popular or easy to use recipes first, and communicate your app's...

The video in this doesn’t show the issue because it only moves recipes by one position with three recipes - it hides the issue. Try testing it with say 15 recipes and moving it by multiple positions at a time and you’ll see that it doesn’t seem to work as one expects.

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there @anon29275264,

Since this is not a bug, but just the way this is working at the moment, I have turned this into a feature request
